expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` before any build/benchmark work.
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'uv run winml inspect -m google/vit-base-patch16-224' to confirm Supported.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite NPU.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'QNN (Qualcomm's NPU EP on Snapdragon X Elite) should be a real option.'

expectations.text: Recommends building once and running `winml perf` twice.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'The clean approach is to build once and then run perf twice, pointing each run at the right artifact for that EP.'

expectations.text: Specifies that CPU run uses optimized (pre-compile) artifact, NOT QNN-compiled.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'The optimized one is your CPU benchmark target; the compiled one is the NPU target.'
Quote: 'The QNN-compiled .onnx is EP-locked -- you cannot run it on CPU and get meaningful numbers.'

expectations.text: Directs to `--help` or uses common patterns; no fabricated flags.
All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
